Hit or Flop? Predicting Chart Success

Spotify Hit Predictor (1960–2019) · 41,103 tracks · Logistic Regression vs Random Forest vs KNN · feature shift across eras
Tracks analyzed
41,103
1960–2019 · 50/50 hit / flop
Best classifier
Random Forest
wins in every era
Peak ROC-AUC
0.92
rises 0.84 → 0.92 over time
Top predictor
instrumentalness
#1 in all eras, rising
Key finding: Hits became more predictable from audio features over time, and the drivers shifted — instrumentalness (vocal presence) and loudness grew steadily more important into the streaming era, while speechiness and acousticness faded and danceability peaked in the CD/Digital era.

5-fold stratified cross-validation. Use the metric selector above.

Feature importance —

Mean |SHAP value| (impact on hit prediction). Switch era above.

Direction of effect —

Standardized logistic-regression coefficient. Green pushes toward HIT, red toward FLOP.

SHAP effect distribution (beeswarm) by era

Each dot is a track. Red = high feature value, blue = low. Right = pushes toward HIT.

All model results

Click a column header to sort. RF rows in green are the best model per subset.
Source: The Spotify Hit Predictor Dataset (1960–2019), Kaggle (theoverman) · Random Forest depth-capped (max_depth=14) for tractable exact TreeSHAP · Generated for academic analysis.
